page0001.text: In a little house with a yellow door lived [Child’s Name]. [He/She] loved many things. Most of all, [he/she] loved saying duas, the beautiful words we say to Allah.
One morning, the sun peeked through the curtains. [Child’s Name] stretched up high. “Good morning!”

page0002.text: [He/She] placed a hand on [his/her] heart and remembered Allah.
“Alhamdu lillahi alladhi ahyana ba’da ma amatana wa ilayhi-n-nushur.” Thank You, Allah, for waking me to a new day.

page0003.text: “Breakfast time!” [Child’s Name] picked up [his/her] spoon. First [he/she] said, “Bismillah.”
Then [he/she] ran to Mama. “May I please go out and play?”

page0004.text: Mama smiled. “Of course. But what do we say first?”
[Child’s Name] knew. “Bismillahi tawakkaltu ‘ala-llah, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah.” I put my trust in Allah.

page0005.text: Up, up went the swing, almost to the clouds! [Child’s Name] looked at the wide blue sky. “SubhanAllah!”
At the masjid, [he/she] stepped softly inside. “Allahumma-ftah li abwaba rahmatik.” Open Your doors of mercy, Allah.

page0006.text: A new morning came. [Child’s Name] hopped out of bed, ready for the day.
“Alhamdu lillahi alladhi kasani hadha.” Thank You, Allah, for my clothes.

page0007.text: [Child’s Name] looked in the mirror and saw [his/her] lovely [eye color] eyes. “Allahumma kama hassanta khalqi fa-hassin khuluqi.” Make my heart beautiful too, Allah.
When [he/she] heard some unkind words, [Child’s Name] knew just what to say. “A’udhu billahi mina-sh-shaytani-r-rajim.”

page0008.text: Saying duas filled [Child’s Name]’s heart with peace and made [him/her] feel close to Allah.
That evening, [he/she] and Mama watched the sunset together. “Now is a beautiful time for a dua,” said Mama.

page0009.text: [Child’s Name] closed [his/her] eyes. “Allahumma bika amsayna wa bika asbahna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilayka-l-masir.”
When the morning sun peeks in again, [Child’s Name] will smile and say, “Alhamdulillah.” Another day of beautiful duas begins.

page0010.text: It was time for school. [Child’s Name] held [his/her] backpack close. “Bismillahi alladhi la yadurru ma’asmihi shay’un fil-ardi wa la fis-sama’i wa huwa-s-sami’ul-‘alim.” Keep me safe, Allah.
In class, the teacher asked, “What are you thankful for?” “My duas!” said [Child’s Name] proudly.

page0021.text: At recess, [Child’s Name] played with [his/her] friends. “Let’s begin with Bismillah!” they all cheered.
Back home, happy and a little sleepy, [Child’s Name] heard Mama ask, “Did you say your duas today?” [He/She] nodded with a big smile. “Yes, Mama!”

page0022.text: Mama gave [him/her] a warm hug. “I am so proud of you, my little star. Duas light up our hearts and keep us close to Allah.”
Snuggled in bed, [Child’s Name] whispered, “Bismika-llahumma amutu wa ahya.”

page0023.text: [His/Her] eyes grew sleepy. From morning until night, [Child’s Name]’s days were full of beautiful duas. And Allah was always near.

treasure_chest: الْحَمْدُ لِلّهِ الّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
Alhamdu lillahi alladhi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilayhi-n-nushur
All praise is for Allah, who gave us life after sleep, and to Him is the return.
الْحَمْدُ لِلّهِ الّذِي كَسَانِي هَٰذَا الثَّوْبَ
All praise is for Allah who clothed me with this.
اللّهُمَّ إِنّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْخُبُثِ وَالْخَبَائِثِ
Allahumma inni a'udhu bika mina-l-khubuthi wa-l-khaba'ith
O Allah, I seek refuge in You from all evil and evil-doers.
I ask You for forgiveness, O Allah.
بِسْمِ اللّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللّهِ وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللّهِ
Bismillahi tawakkaltu 'ala-llah, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
In the name of Allah, I trust in Allah; there is no might nor power except with Allah.
بِسْمِ اللّهِ الّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
Bismillahi-lladhi la yadurru ma'a-smihi shay'un fil-ardi wa la fis-sama'i wa huwa-s-sami'ul-'alim
In the name of Allah, with whose name nothing can cause harm. He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
O Allah, open for me the doors of Your mercy.
When I look in the mirror
اللّهُمَّ كَمَا حَسَّنْتَ خَلْقِي فَحَسِّنْ خُلُقِي
Allahumma kama hassanta khalqi fa-hassin khuluqi
O Allah, as You made my form beautiful, make my character beautiful too.
I seek refuge in Allah from Satan, the outcast.
اللّهُمَّ بِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
Allahumma bika amsayna wa bika asbahna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilayka-l-masir
O Allah, by You we enter the evening and morning, by You we live and die, and to You is the return.
In Your name, O Allah, I die and I live.
intro_text: There once lived a wonderful child named [Child’s Name]. [He/She] loved many things, but most of all, [he/she] loved saying duas, the beautiful words we say to Allah, all through the day.

story_spreads.narrative: Soft morning light slipped through the curtains, and [Child’s Name] woke up with a big stretch and a smile.
story_spreads.translit: Alhamdu lillahi alladhi ahyana ba’da ma amatana wa ilayhi-n-nushur.
story_spreads.meaning: Thank You, Allah, for waking me to a new day.

story_spreads.narrative: [Child’s Name] got dressed all on [his/her] own, ready for a brand new day.
story_spreads.meaning: Thank You, Allah, for my clothes.

story_spreads.narrative: “Breakfast time!” [Child’s Name] picked up [his/her] spoon and remembered to begin the right way.
Soon it was time to head outside. [Child’s Name] paused at the door for a special dua.
story_spreads.translit: Bismillahi tawakkaltu ‘ala-llah, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah.
story_spreads.meaning: I put my trust in Allah.

story_spreads.narrative: Up, up went the swing, almost to the clouds! [Child’s Name] gazed at the wide blue sky.
Inside the masjid, [Child’s Name] stepped softly and quietly.
story_spreads.meaning: O Allah, open the doors of Your mercy.

story_spreads.occasion: When I look in the mirror
story_spreads.narrative: [Child’s Name] looked in the mirror and saw [his/her] bright [eye color] eyes.
story_spreads.translit: Allahumma kama hassanta khalqi fa-hassin khuluqi.
story_spreads.meaning: Make my heart beautiful too, Allah.

story_spreads.narrative: When [Child’s Name] heard some unkind words, [he/she] knew just what to say.
That evening, [Child’s Name] and Mama watched the sky turn soft and gold.
story_spreads.translit: Allahumma bika amsayna wa bika asbahna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilayka-l-masir.
story_spreads.meaning: O Allah, by You we enter the evening.

story_spreads.narrative: As the first stars appeared, [Child’s Name] closed [his/her] eyes, thankful for a beautiful day.
Snuggled warm under the blanket, [Child’s Name] whispered one last dua of the day.
story_spreads.meaning: In Your name, O Allah, I live and die.

story_spreads.narrative: From morning until night, [Child’s Name]’s days were full of beautiful duas. And Allah was always near.
story_spreads.verse_arabic: وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِي عَنِّي فَإِنِّي قَرِيبٌ
story_spreads.verse_english: “And when My servants ask about Me, indeed I am near.”
